headline: HYCROFT SITS ON $189M CASH, ZERO DEBT — M&I Gold Resources Jump 55% to 16.4Moz in Q1 Filing
snippet: Hycroft Mining's Q1 2026 10-Q reveals a $189M cash position with no debt and a 55% jump in measured and indicated resources to 16.4 million oz gold and 562.6 million oz silver at its Nevada flagship—one of the largest known silver deposits in the US. The company continues to expand its resource base with an active drill program.

headline: SERABI GOLD M&I GROWS 29% to 731,000 oz at 9.1 g/t — Reserves Up 55% at Brazilian Palito Complex
snippet: Serabi Gold's updated resource estimate shows consolidated M&I gold resources of 731koz at 9.1 g/t—a 29% increase year-over-year—while proven and probable reserves at the Palito Complex surged 55% to 228koz at 6.2 g/t. The inferred resource now stands at 653koz. Serabi operates debt-free in Brazil's Pará state with record gold production forecast for 2026.

headline: SILVER SLIDES to $74 as Solar Giants Ditch the Metal for Copper — Sixth Straight Deficit Year Cushions the Fall
snippet: Silver fell to the $73-74/oz range on April 28 as the dollar surged and LongI and Jinko Solar announced large-scale copper-cell solar panel production—reducing silver PV demand by an estimated 7% year-over-year. However, the Silver Institute forecasts a 67-million-oz supply deficit in 2026 (sixth consecutive year), with physical investment demand up 20% to cushion miner cash flows.

headline: HONEY BADGER SILVER Closes 240Moz AgEq Prairie Creek Deal — Fully Permitted NWT Underground Project
snippet: Honey Badger Silver (TSXV: TUF) closed its acquisition of Canadian Zinc's Prairie Creek Project in the NWT for C$10M cash plus C$2M in shares and warrants. The PC Silver Project hosts a historical resource of ~240 million oz silver equivalent (M&I) and ~167Moz AgEq inferred at one of the highest-grade, fully permitted underground silver projects in a Tier-1 jurisdiction.

headline: HELIOSTAR CLOSES Goldstrike Gold Project from Liberty Gold for US$72.5M — 975koz Indicated in Utah
snippet: Heliostar Metals closed the purchase of Liberty Gold's Goldstrike project in southern Utah for US$72.5M total (US$10M cash at close, US$2.5M in shares, US$60M in milestones). Goldstrike hosts 975,000 indicated oz at 0.46 g/t gold plus 90koz inferred—adding a US asset to Heliostar's growing Mexico gold production base.

headline: USA RARE EARTH's $2.8 BILLION BRAZIL BET — Serra Verde Locks Up All 4 Magnetic Rare Earths Outside China
snippet: Motley Fool analyzes USA Rare Earth's (USAR) $2.8B acquisition of Serra Verde's Pela Ema mine in Brazil, which produces all four critical magnetic rare earths—neodymium, praseodymium, terbium, dysprosium—under a 100% US-government-backed offtake covering Phase 1 output. The article weighs whether the 13% post-announcement stock pop still leaves upside at current prices.

headline: ATICO MINING Q1: GOLD PRODUCTION SURGES 36% to 2,108 oz While Copper Slips 6% at Colombian Mine
snippet: Atico Mining (TSXV: ATY) produced 2.09 million lbs copper and 2,108 oz gold in Q1 2026 at its El Roble mine in Colombia—gold jumped 36% year-over-year while copper slipped 6% on lower grades. Mill throughput averaged 734 tpd at 1.86% Cu and 1.69 g/t Au. The CEO noted visible improvement in Q2 operational performance.

headline: LAFLEUR MINERALS GRABS GROUND Adjacent to Fresnillo's 397koz Val-d'Or Deposit; Swanson Drill Hits 86.8 g/t Au
snippet: LaFleur Minerals (CSE: LFLR) acquired 46 claims covering 1,781 hectares contiguous with Fresnillo's McKenzie Break deposit (146koz indicated + 250koz inferred at 3.1-3.2 g/t) in Val-d'Or, Québec. Drilling at the nearby Swanson project returned localised high-grade hits including 86.8 g/t Au over 1.0m at ~300-450m depth, outside the current resource boundary.

headline: GOLD'S SAFE-HAVEN NARRATIVE IS BROKEN — But the Bull Market Thesis Isn't (Money Metals)
snippet: Money Metals Exchange argues that gold is currently trading as a risk-on asset, not a safe haven, because Western traders are fixated on Fed rate policy and dollar strength—not geopolitics. Once the Iran conflict moves to the rearview mirror, the structural drivers of the gold bull market (central bank buying, de-dollarisation, inflation) will reassert themselves.
